etymology.description: 學(學) (xué, "to learn") + 校(校) (xiào, "school") = place of learning -> school
etymology.interpretation: 學(學) (xué, "to learn") depicts a child under a roof, symbolizing receiving knowledge or education. 校(校) (xiào, "school") originally referred to a place of examination or comparison, where standards are upheld. Together, they create the concept of a structured environment or institution dedicated to learning and education.

examples.chinese: 我们每天早上八点去学校(我們每天早上八點去學校)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen měitiān zǎoshang bā diǎn qù xuéxiào.
examples.english: We go to school at eight every morning.

examples.chinese: 学校的图书馆很大(學校的圖書館很大)。
examples.english: The school's library is very big.

examples.chinese: 放学后(放學後)，我通常和朋友打篮球(我通常和朋友打籃球)。
examples.pinyin: Fàngxué hòu, wǒ tōngcháng hé péngyǒu dǎ lánqiú.
examples.english: After school, I usually play basketball with friends.
